data.title: Brother Machi closed his long positions in Bitcoin and held long positions in Ethereum worth approximately $31.6 million.
data.description: Hyperbot data shows that Huang Licheng, nicknamed "Brother Machi," closed all his Bitcoin long positions 30 minutes ago. He currently holds Ethereum long positions with 25x leverage, but is continuously reducing his holdings. His current position is 14,250 ETH, with a value of approximately $31.6 million.

data.title: Trump: The Iranian Navy has been completely destroyed.
data.description: US President Trump stated that the Iranian navy (158 ships in total) has sunk to the bottom of the sea and been completely destroyed. What we haven't yet targeted are their few so-called "fast attack boats" because we don't consider them a significant threat. Warning: If any of these vessels approach our blockade line, we will immediately eliminate them using the same methods we use against drug trafficking vessels at sea. What we haven't yet targeted are their few so-called "fast attack boats" because we don't consider them a significant threat. This will be swift and brutal. Currently, 98.2% of drugs entering the United States by sea or sea route have been stopped. (Jinshi)

data.title: Binance Wallet Delays GENIUS Airdrop Launch to 11 PM
data.description: Binance Wallet has delayed the GENIUS token airdrop collection time to 11 PM, according to a post on its official X account.

data.title: ECB Highlights Tokenization's Potential in European Markets
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) has emphasized that tokenization could enhance European capital markets, provided it is supported by central bank money, interoperable infrastructure, and suitable regulation. According to NS3.AI, the ECB's macroprudential report, released on April 13, suggests that Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) could bolster savings and investment functions within the European Union. The report notes that the potential benefits of DLT are contingent upon how infrastructure and policy authorities manage emerging risks.

data.title: American Bankers Association Warns of Stablecoin Interest Impact on Regional Banks
data.description: The American Bankers Association (ABA) has issued a warning regarding the potential impact of allowing interest payments on stablecoins. According to NS3.AI, the ABA expressed concerns that such a move could lead to a withdrawal of deposits from regional banks, subsequently reducing local lending capabilities. This warning comes in response to a report by the White House Council of Economic Advisers, which suggested that prohibiting interest on stablecoin holdings would not have a significant effect on regional banks. The ABA's caution highlights the ongoing debate over the regulation of stablecoins and their potential implications for the traditional banking sector.

data.title: AI-Driven Deluthium Protocol Launches On-Chain Dark Pool for Institutional Users
data.description:
Deluthium Protocol has announced the official launch of its on-chain dark pool execution layer, Deluthium Dark, aimed at institutional and professional users. According to Foresight News, this execution environment ensures that trading intentions do not enter the public memory pool, allowing for matching and settlement in a protected environment. This approach fundamentally eliminates the risks of front-running and sandwich attacks.

The underlying execution engine is powered by a self-optimizing reinforcement learning system, which intelligently splits large orders based on real-time market conditions and continuously optimizes execution quality through closed-loop feedback from each transaction.

Deluthium Dark offers five key product capabilities: order confidentiality, intelligent algorithm execution, deterministic pricing by institutional market makers, a fair access mechanism, and auditable atomic settlement. Deluthium positions itself as an AI-native fintech infrastructure company, dedicated to providing foundational infrastructure for artificial intelligence in the digital finance sector through its reinforcement learning intelligent execution system.

data.title: Oil Prices Drop Amid Reports of Iran's Potential Nuclear Concession
data.description: Oil prices for both Brent and WTI have experienced a sharp decline, dropping nearly $3 each. According to Odaily, the current prices are $100.51 per barrel for Brent and $95.94 per barrel for WTI. Reports suggest that Iranian officials are considering halting uranium enrichment activities as a condition for the United States to end ongoing conflicts.

data.title: U.S. Department of Justice Initiates Compensation Process for OneCoin Scam Victims
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice has announced the commencement of the compensation application process for victims of the OneCoin cryptocurrency scam. According to Foresight News, the department has initiated criminal forfeiture proceedings against the proceeds of the crime, with the net proceeds to be used for victim compensation. Victims who purchased OneCoin between 2014 and 2019 and suffered net losses can submit compensation applications via onecoinremission.com by June 30, 2026, without incurring any fees or needing legal representation.

OneCoin was co-founded in 2014 by Bulgarian Ruja Ignatova, known as the 'Crypto Queen,' and Karl Sebastian Greenwood. The scheme involved selling fraudulent cryptocurrency education packages, enticing global investors to purchase OneCoin tokens, which never operated on any blockchain. The estimated fraud amount ranges from $4 billion to $15 billion, making it one of the largest cryptocurrency scams in history. Ignatova mysteriously disappeared in 2017 and remains one of the FBI's most wanted fugitives. Greenwood was sentenced to 20 years in prison in 2023.

data.title: Binance Wallet: GENIUS airdrop launch time delayed to 11 PM
data.description: Binance Wallet posted on the X platform that the GENIUS airdrop claim time has been postponed to 11 PM.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 600 USDT with a 1.54% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 13, 2026, 14:00 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 600 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 600.090027 USDT, with a narrowed 1.54% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: The US military's blockade order on Iranian ports has expired.
data.description: As previously announced by US President Trump, the US military's blockade of Iranian ports was scheduled to take effect at 10:00 AM Eastern Time on the 13th (10:00 PM Beijing Time). (Xinhua)

data.title: U.S. March Existing Home Sales Fall Short of Expectations
data.description: The annualized total of existing home sales in the United States for March was reported at 3.98 million units. According to Jin10, this figure fell short of the anticipated 4.06 million units. The previous value was revised from 4.09 million units to 4.13 million units.

data.title: SEC Provides Temporary Guidance for DeFi Front-Ends and Wallet Apps
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's (SEC) Division of Trading and Markets has issued temporary guidance for certain decentralized finance (DeFi) front-ends, wallet extensions, and mobile applications. According to NS3.AI, these platforms may operate without broker-dealer registration for a period of five years, unless further action is taken by the SEC.

The guidance specifies that these platforms must adhere to certain conditions, including refraining from order routing, providing investment advice, or holding custody of user assets. Additionally, they must implement fixed, neutral fee structures. This guidance is particularly relevant for self-custodial wallet interfaces, which allow users to maintain control over their own assets.

The SEC's decision aims to provide clarity and a framework for DeFi platforms to operate within regulatory boundaries while ensuring investor protection. This move is seen as a step towards accommodating the evolving landscape of decentralized finance and its associated technologies.

data.title: Oncoclinicas Seeks Temporary Court Protection Amid Financial Struggles
data.description: Oncoclinicas announced plans to file for temporary court protection in Brazil on Monday due to increasing financial challenges. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's decision as it grapples with mounting pressures. The move comes as Oncoclinicas seeks to navigate its financial difficulties and stabilize its operations. The company aims to restructure its debts and secure its future amid the current economic climate. This step is part of a broader strategy to address its financial situation and ensure continued service delivery.

data.title: Ondo Finance Seeks SEC Approval for Tokenized Securities
data.description:
Ondo Finance has submitted a No-Action Letter application to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) regarding its Ondo Global Markets (OGM) product. According to Foresight News, the application is limited in scope, maintaining the OGM product's focus on providing tokenized notes for U.S. stocks and ETFs to non-U.S. investors. The underlying securities and official ledger records will remain within the existing custody system, held by BitGo.

The main change involves recording related securities interests in tokenized form on the Ethereum mainnet under limited circumstances. This aims to enhance collateral monitoring, subscription and redemption processes, and reconciliation operations.

Ondo Finance emphasizes that this move is an operational improvement to the existing product rather than a restructuring of the current market framework. A No-Action Letter is an informal regulatory tool used by the SEC, allowing companies to seek confirmation that SEC staff will not recommend enforcement action for new products or models. If granted, it provides regulatory tacit approval to proceed with business before formal legislative procedures are completed.

data.title: Bernstein: The Bitcoin market has already priced in quantum risks and they do not pose a short-term existential threat.
data.description: Bernstein stated that the previous Bitcoin pullback had already reflected market concerns about the risks of quantum computing to some extent, believing that the threat is "real but manageable" and not yet an imminent systemic risk. Although research, including from Google, suggests that future quantum computers may be able to quickly crack commonly used blockchain encryption algorithms in extreme cases, advancements in zero-knowledge proofs and quantum-resistant cryptography have provided some degree of protection. Bernstein believes that Bitcoin developers still have approximately 3 to 5 years to advance the quantum-resistant upgrade path, mentioning the current BIP-360 proposal, which can reduce some of the quantum exposure risk through soft forks. Furthermore, institutional investors (including ETF issuers and corporate holders) are expected to play an active role in driving upgrade consensus. However, industry experts point out that the main challenge of quantum-resistant upgrades lies in user migration and consensus formation, rather than the technical implementation itself. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Goldman Sachs CEO: It's too early to judge the impact of rising energy prices and inflation on economic growth.
data.description: Goldman Sachs CEO Solomon stated that it is too early to assess the impact of rising energy prices and inflation on economic growth. Furthermore, concerns about AI-driven disruption in the software industry are weighing on market sentiment. As the first quarter progresses, macroeconomic uncertainty is intensifying, prompting clients to actively adjust their portfolio allocations. (Jinshi)

data.title: AI TRENDS | Claude Opus 4.6 Drops in Accuracy on BridgeBench Retest
data.description: BridgeMind reported a significant decline in the performance of Claude Opus 4.6 during a recent retest on the BridgeBench platform. According to NS3.AI, the AI model's accuracy fell from 83.3% to 68.3%, causing its ranking to drop from second to tenth on the hallucination leaderboard. Critics have pointed out that the comparison may be flawed, as the original score was based on six tasks, while the retest involved 30 tasks.

data.title: Netherlands Financial Regulator Warns of Private Credit Market Turmoil
data.description: The Netherlands' financial markets regulator has issued a warning about potential further disruptions in the $1.8 trillion private credit market. Bloomberg posted on X that this caution comes in the wake of recent turmoil experienced by some direct lenders. The regulator highlighted concerns over the stability of the market, emphasizing the need for vigilance among investors and stakeholders. The private credit sector, which has grown significantly in recent years, faces challenges that could impact its future performance. The warning underscores the importance of monitoring developments within this sector to mitigate risks and ensure financial stability.

data.title: The US SEC issued a statement providing key guidance: some crypto trading platforms do not require broker registration.
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Division of Trading and Markets issued a staff statement providing guidance on whether certain covered user interfaces (CUIs) used to generate trading orders for crypto-asset securities need to be registered as broker-dealers. The statement indicates that, under certain conditions, providers of such interfaces may not need to register as broker-dealers under Section 15 of the Securities Exchange Act. These conditions include: not soliciting specific trades, not providing investment advice, not controlling or executing trades, generating trading orders solely based on objective parameters, and fully disclosing to users their fee structure, potential conflicts of interest, and related risks. The SEC emphasizes that such interfaces typically exist as websites, browser plugins, or wallet applications, used to convert user-defined trading parameters into on-chain executable instructions, while also providing market data such as prices, paths, and fees. Furthermore, the statement clarifies that this exemption does not apply to activities such as participating in trade matching, fund custody, order routing, or providing investment advice. This guidance is interim and will automatically expire five years after April 13, 2026, unless further action is taken. The U.S. SEC stated that this move aims to provide a clearer regulatory framework for activities related to crypto asset securities and to continue to solicit market feedback.
